# Team Management

A successful robotics team needs more than good robots — it needs good organization, clear responsibilities, and proper documentation. This section covers everything from weekly duties to tournament management.

::: {.callout-note}
## Learning Objectives

By the end of this section, you will be able to:

1. Manage weekly team responsibilities effectively
2. Execute tournament day duties without stress
3. Maintain an engineering notebook that impresses judges
4. Build a culture of continuous improvement
:::

---

# Section 6: Team Management {.unnumbered}

## 6.1 Weekly Responsibilities

Every team member has duties beyond robot building and coding.

### Role-Based Responsibilities

#### Captain Duties

| Day | Task | Duration |
|-----|------|----------|
| **Monday** | Review previous week's progress | 15 min |
| **Monday** | Set week's goals and priorities | 15 min |
| **Monday** | Check in with each team member | 10 min |
| **Thursday** | Run strategy session | 30 min |
| **Thursday** | Review and update notebook | 20 min |
| **Ongoing** | Mediate conflicts, maintain morale | As needed |

#### Builder Duties

| Day | Task | Duration |
|-----|------|----------|
| **Monday** | Inspect robot for damage | 10 min |
| **Monday** | Document current state | 5 min |
| **Monday** | Complete assigned build tasks | 2-3 hrs |
| **Thursday** | Implement any code-driven changes | 30 min |
| **Thursday** | Test and verify changes | 20 min |
| **Ongoing** | Maintain tool organization | Ongoing |

#### Coder Duties

| Day | Task | Duration |
|-----|------|----------|
| **Monday** | Pull latest code changes | 5 min |
| **Monday** | Review autonomous performance | 15 min |
| **Monday** | Implement new features | 1-2 hrs |
| **Thursday** | Test autonomous routines | 45 min |
| **Thursday** | Update code documentation | 15 min |
| **Ongoing** | Maintain code repository | Ongoing |

#### Driver Duties

| Day | Task | Duration |
|-----|------|----------|
| **Monday** | Practice basic maneuvers | 30 min |
| **Monday** | Provide feedback on robot handling | 10 min |
| **Thursday** | Run practice matches | 60 min |
| **Thursday** | Work on timing and precision | 20 min |
| **Ongoing** | Study match video for improvement | As needed |

### Weekly Meeting Structure

```
MRJL Weekly Meeting Template
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

MONDAY MEETING (18:00 - 21:00)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
18:00 - 18:15   │ Arrive, settle, equipment check
                │
18:15 - 18:30   │ Captain's briefing
                │ - Last week's progress review
                │ - This week's goals
                │ - Role assignments
                │
18:30 - 20:45   │ WORK SESSION
                │ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
                │ │ 18:30 - 19:30  │ Build block           │
                │ │ 19:30 - 19:45  │ Break                  │
                │ │ 19:45 - 20:45  │ Code & integration     │
                │ └─────────────────────────────────────────┘
                │
20:45 - 21:00   │ Wrap-up
                │ - What was accomplished
                │ - What's next
                │ - Clean workspace

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

THURSDAY MEETING (18:00 - 20:00)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
18:00 - 18:15   │ Arrive, robot inspection
                │
18:15 - 18:45   │ Strategy session
                │ - Review match footage
                │ - Discuss opponent strategies
                │ - Practice run planning
                │
18:45 - 19:45   │ Practice Matches
                │ - 3-4 full run-throughs
                │ - Driver handoff practice
                │ - Endgame timing
                │
19:45 - 20:00   │ Notebook update
                │ - Document today's learnings
                │ - Note any problems solved
                │
20:00           │ Clean and dismiss

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```

## 6.2 Tournament Responsibilities

Tournament days require extra organization and focus.

### Pre-Tournament Week Checklist

#### One Week Before

- [ ] Confirm registration and payment
- [ ] Review tournament schedule
- [ ] Check robot for any needed repairs
- [ ] Charge all batteries
- [ ] Print team materials (resume, pit display)

#### Three Days Before

- [ ] Confirm transportation arrangements
- [ ] Review match schedule
- [ ] Prepare spare parts kit
- [ ] Update emergency contacts

#### Day Before

- [ ] Pack robot and tools
- [ ] Pack batteries and charger
- [ ] Pack notebook and team materials
- [ ] Charge personal devices
- [ ] Review tournament rules

### Tournament Day Timeline

```
Tournament Day Timeline
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

05:30  │ Wake up / Final packing
06:30  │ Depart for venue
07:30  │ Arrive at venue
       │
07:30 - 08:00  │ Unload and set up pit
08:00 - 09:00  │ Inspection queue
09:00 - 09:30  │ Opening ceremonies
09:30 - 12:00  │ Practice matches & Robot skills
12:00 - 13:00  │ Lunch break
13:00 - 17:00  │ Qualifier matches
17:00 - 18:00  │ Alliance selection (if applicable)
18:00 - 19:00  │ Elimination matches
19:00 - 20:00  │ Awards ceremony
20:00 - 21:00  │ Pack up and depart

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```

### Pit Crew Responsibilities

During tournament matches, not everyone can be at the field.

#### Pit Crew Roles

| Role | Location | Responsibilities |
|------|---------|-----------------|
| **Pit Manager** | Pit station | Coordinates all pit activities, manages time |
| **Robot Tech** | Pit station | Quick repairs, battery swaps, adjustments |
| **Data Analyst** | Pit or stands | Watches matches, records opponent info |
| **Runner** | Between pit and field | Brings robot to/from field queue |

#### During Your Match

```
Pit Crew Actions During Match
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

DURING YOUR MATCH:
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
• Pit Manager watches match from pit
• Robot Tech has tools ready for quick adjustments
• DO NOT approach the field during matches
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

AFTER YOUR MATCH (if time before next queue call):
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
• Runner retrieves robot from field
• Robot Tech inspects for damage
• Any quick repairs are made
• Battery is swapped to fresh
• Robot Tech confirms robot ready
• Runner brings robot back to pit
• Robot is placed in queue area
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

⚠️ You have approximately 10 minutes between matches
   to prepare for your next match
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```

### Field Crew Responsibilities

The drivers and captain are at the field during matches.

#### Driver Station Setup

```
Driver Station Checklist
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

AT THE FIELD (30 minutes before first match):
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
□ Controller batteries (if applicable)
□ Controller configured to team number
□ Controller tested with robot
□ Any driver aids / markings applied
□ Timekeeper briefed
□ Strategy card ready
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

BEFORE EACH MATCH:
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
□ Robot powered on in inspection area
□ Gyro / sensors calibrated
□ All mechanisms verified working
□ Fresh battery confirmed
□ Autonomous mode selected
□ Controller connected
□ "Ready" confirmed to captain
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```

## 6.3 Notebooking

The engineering notebook is your team's documentation of the engineering design process. Judges use it to understand your team's journey and decision-making.

### What Goes in the Notebook

::: {.callout-tip}
## The Golden Rule of Notebooking

**If it's not in the notebook, it didn't happen.**

Document everything, even the failures. Judges value the story of how you got to your final design, not just the final design itself.
:::

#### Daily Entries Should Include

| Category | What to Document |
|----------|-----------------|
| **Progress** | What was built or coded today |
| **Decisions** | Why you chose one design over another |
| **Problems** | Issues encountered and how you solved them |
| **Measurements** | Actual data (times, distances, scores) |
| **Next Steps** | What's planned for the next session |

#### Weekly Summaries

```
Weekly Summary Template
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Week of: _______________
Team Members Present: _______________

ACCOMPLISHMENTS THIS WEEK:
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1. _______________________________________________________
2. _______________________________________________________
3. _______________________________________________________

PROBLEMS ENCOUNTERED:
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Problem 1: _________________________________________________
Solution: __________________________________________________

CHANGES MADE TO DESIGN:
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Before → After: ____________________________________________
Reason for change: _________________________________________

GOALS FOR NEXT WEEK:
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1. _______________________________________________________
2. _______________________________________________________

METRICS:
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Autonomous success rate: ____%
Driver run time: ____ seconds
Match score average: ____ points

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```

### Notebook Organization

| Section | Content | Page Range |
|---------|---------|------------|
| **Cover Page** | Team info, season, contact | 1 |
| **Table of Contents** | Overview of sections | 2-3 |
| **Team Introduction** | Members, roles, goals | 4-5 |
| **Brainstorming** | Initial ideas and sketches | 6-15 |
| **Design Process** | How you chose your design | 16-30 |
| **Building Log** | Construction photos and notes | 31-60 |
| **Code Development** | Programming evolution | 61-80 |
| **Testing & Iteration** | Results and improvements | 81-100 |
| **Competition Records** | Match results, notes | 101-120 |
| **Team Reflections** | What you learned | 121+ |

### Photo Documentation

Take photos of:

- [ ] Initial brainstorming sketches
- [ ] Prototype versions (even failed ones)
- [ ] Assembly progress
- [ ] Testing setups
- [ ] Competition day
- [ ] Team members working

::: {.callout-example}
## Photo Caption Example

**Bad caption**: "Robot building"

**Good caption**: 
> "Week 3: Testing the first arm prototype. We discovered that the 5:1 gear ratio provided enough torque but was too slow for our target cycle time of 4 seconds. Switched to 3:1 ratio in Week 4."

**Great caption**:
> "Arm prototype v1.0 (left) vs v2.0 (right). The main change was increasing gear ratio from 5:1 to 3:1, reducing cycle time from 5.2s to 3.8s. Trade-off: we had to reinforce the mount point because the faster arm stressed the beam joint. Added double-support brackets which added 15g but solved the flex issue."
:::

### Judging Criteria

Engineering notebooks are typically judged on:

| Criterion | Weight | What Judges Look For |
|-----------|--------|---------------------|
| **Documentation** | 30% | Completeness of process documentation |
| **Design Process** | 25% | Evidence of iteration and improvement |
| **Team Journey** | 20% | Story of how the team worked together |
| **Engineering** | 15% | Technical rigor and problem-solving |
| **Presentation** | 10% | Organization and readability |

::: {.callout-tip}
## Notebook Tips

1. **Write weekly, not all at once** — It's easier and more accurate
2. **Include failures** — They're often more educational than successes
3. **Use sketches** — A quick drawing explains more than paragraphs
4. **Be specific** — "Changed arm design" vs "Changed arm mounting from single-screw to double-screw for increased rigidity"
5. **Document decisions** — Not just what you did, but why you chose it
:::
